project: Lawless Design Swiss rakkan-in lion and sphere seal
purpose: Create a refined Swiss house seal for the bottom of the homepage, functioning as a maker's mark, authorship seal, and heritage emblem.

core_concept.description: A Swiss reinterpretation of a rakkan-in / hanko seal, using the formal logic of a carved square artist stamp but expressed through Zurich restraint, European heraldic dignity, and the Lawless Design black sphere.
should_look_like: seal worthy of embossing, stamping, or printed collateral
should_not_look_like: literal Japanese seal with Japanese motifs
core_concept.design_philosophy: The spirit should be rakkan-in, but the form should be Swiss, proprietary, restrained, and institutional.

format.orientation: Square seal with horizontal lion composition inside

outer_border.description: A square outline border with subtly softened, hand-carved corners. The border is essential to giving the mark its rakkan-in seal authority.

thickness.relative_weight: Slightly heavier than most inner lines
hierarchy: Inner anatomical lines lightest and fewest
requirements: Stable enough to hold the seal together
Feels like the perimeter cut of a proper seal face

allowed: minor evidence of tool movement at corners

relationship_to_interior.description: The lion should sit inside the square with deliberate tension. It should feel embedded in the seal, not floating inside a box.
requirements: Interior elements close enough to feel integrated
Enough margin to avoid cramped feeling
Mane or tail may come near the border to create carved seal compression
Sphere must not touch the border

overall_structure_inside_square.description: The composition should be treated as compressed seal architecture rather than an illustration placed inside a frame.
spatial_arrangement: Lion runs horizontally across the lower two-thirds of the square
Head lifts into the upper-left or upper-central area, while still facing right
Body lies elongated, grounded, and calm
Sphere sits low and forward under the front paw
Tail curves back in a controlled way to complete the right side
Square border contains all elements and gives the mark stamp authority
visual_priority: Internal carved line rhythms such as mane, face, shoulder, and limb separations
overall_structure_inside_square.first_read: The mark should read as one coherent seal, not as separate lion, sphere, and border components.

subject.attitude: Old guardian at ease, mature, calm, authoritative, dignified

body.description: The lion reclines horizontally in a long, calm, aged posture, adapted to the logic of a carved square stamp.
requirements: One paw resting on the sphere
Hindquarters folded in a calm settled way
Latent power rather than active movement

front_legs.description: Both forelegs extend forward. One front paw rests on the sphere, while the other remains grounded to form the horizontal base.
front_legs.grounded_paw: Relaxed, refined, and supportive, not oversized or cartoon-like.

hindquarters.description: Rear body folded naturally into the reclining posture with enough weight to imply maturity and age.

tail.description: Tail curves back in a controlled and elegant manner, helping complete the right side of the square.
requirements: Can approach the border without touching it if compositionally useful

head_and_expression.position: Held upright and slightly proud, but not tilted too high

mane.description: The mane is a key place for the rakkan-in carving character. It should be stylised as carved flowing masses rather than naturalistic hair.
treatment: Outer contour of mane should be bold
Inner separations should be few and deliberate
Mane should frame the face strongly
Mane should flow into the chest
Use broad controlled shapes like elegant channels cut into a stamp

sphere.description: The sphere is the existing brand symbol and should remain the only truly absolute form inside the seal.
sphere.outline: No outline unless required for reproduction

placement.position: Low and slightly forward, under the lion's front paw
placement.margin: Comfortably inside the border with breathing room
avoid: appearing like a toy or ball

line_logic.principle: Every visible black line should feel like the result of a controlled cut into material.
linework_style: balanced between positive and negative space
premium hand-carved rather than rough handmade

outer_contour_lines.description: Lion outer contour should be clean, confident, and slightly weighty.

inner_lines.description: Inner lines should be sparse and disciplined.
allowed: one or two lines for the mane
one line for shoulder or chest transition
one line for hindquarter mass if needed
slight organic taper at line ends
tiny evidence of tool turns in corners

positive_negative_space.description: The enclosed square should function as a field of black and white balance, giving the mark true rakkan-in flavour.
requirements: Enough white space for air and dignity
Enough black structure for weight and authority
Clear rhythm between dense and open areas

interior_margin.description: Modest margin between border and drawing.

positioning: Head in upper-left to mid-left zone while facing right
Body runs horizontally across the centre and lower two-thirds
Tail completes right lower or mid-right zone
Mane and tail may approach border to integrate the image
composition.homepage_fit: The seal should be compact enough for a homepage footer while retaining authority and legibility.

surface_character.description: Even if the production asset is crisp, it should anticipate the feel of a real stamped impression.

desired_feeling: old enough to feel like it has always existed
avoid: Japanese characters or explicit Japanese motifs

success_criteria: Reads first as one coherent square seal
Feels Swiss, restrained, and proprietary rather than Japanese-literal
Looks like a mark of authorship for a high-status design house
The lion is mature, calm, elongated, reclining, and right-facing
The head is upright and noble without being theatrical
The paw rests on the sphere with calm authority
The sphere remains a perfect solid black brand symbol
The border feels hand-carved with subtly softened corners
The mark would work as a real one-colour stamp, emboss, or homepage footer seal
literal_final_image_description: A compact square seal with a black outline border and subtly softened hand-carved corners. Inside it, a right-facing lion reclines in an elongated, elegant posture, occupying most of the lower field. Its head is upright and noble, its mane is stylised into restrained carved forms, and one front paw rests with calm authority on a perfect black sphere placed low in front. The whole composition is balanced, quiet, and seal-like, with strong black linework, refined negative space, and the feeling of a master-carved Swiss house emblem designed to stamp authorship onto finished work.
condensed_prompt: A master-carved Swiss rakkan-in style square seal with slightly softened corners, containing a right-facing elongated reclining lion in noble repose, its paw resting calmly on a perfect solid black sphere. Rendered with disciplined black lines, restrained inner carving, balanced negative space, and the quiet authority of an inherited Zurich house mark. Black only, transparent or white background, premium stamp-like finish, not cartoon, not mascot, not distressed.
